message:
fix(interactive): replace try_send with backpressure-aware enqueue for extension host action events

ext_session.rs:
- Replaced all event_tx.try_send() calls in InteractiveExtensionHostActions
  with enqueue_pi_event() which awaits channel capacity instead of
  silently dropping messages when the bounded channel is full. This
  prevents display notes, continue-turn triggers, and user message
  enqueues from being lost under backpressure — a scenario that occurs
  when the event processing loop is temporarily busy during streaming
  or compaction.
- Moved Cx::current() acquisition earlier in send_message() so it is
  available for both the streaming and idle code paths.
- Added configurable-capacity test harness builder
  (build_host_actions_with_capacity) to enable backpressure testing.
- Added three backpressure tests:
  streaming_send_message_preserves_display_note_under_backpressure,
  idle_send_message_preserves_display_and_continue_under_backpressure,
  idle_send_user_message_preserves_text_under_backpressure.
- Updated .beads/issues.jsonl with current bead status entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1d3","title":"Implement TUI snapshot testing infrastructure","description":"# Implement TUI snapshot testing infrastructure

## Goal
Create infrastructure for testing the interactive TUI by capturing terminal output
and comparing against golden snapshots, with detailed logging.

## Background
src/interactive.rs is 2842 lines with only 2 trivial tests (<0.1% coverage).
This is the most user-visible code and needs comprehensive testing.

## Approach: Snapshot Testing

### Why Snapshots?
- TUI output is complex (ANSI codes, layout)
- Pixel-perfect testing impractical
- Snapshots capture "expected output" simply
- Easy to update when intentional changes made

### Snapshot Format
```
// tests/snapshots/tui_initial_state.snap
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Pi - AI Coding Agent                │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                     │
│ > [cursor]                          │
│                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Model: claude-sonnet-4   Tokens: 0  │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for each snapshot test.
- Log view size, theme name, state flags, and snapshot name.
- On mismatch, dump the rendered view (ANSI-stripped) + diff context.

## Determinism Requirements
- Force terminal size (80x24) and disable time-based animations/spinners in test mode.
- Strip ANSI codes for snapshots.
- Use a fixed default theme and locale.

## Implementation

### Snapshot Library
Use insta crate for Rust snapshot testing:
```toml
[dev-dependencies]
insta = { version = "1", features = ["filters"] }
```

### Test Structure
```rust
// tests/tui_snapshot.rs

use insta::assert_snapshot;
use pi::interactive::PiApp;

#[test]
fn test_initial_state() {
    let app = PiApp::new(Config::default());
    let view = app.view();
    assert_snapshot!("initial_state", strip_ansi(&view));
}
```

### ANSI Code Handling
Strip ANSI codes for deterministic snapshots:
```rust
fn strip_ansi(s: &str) -> String {
    let re = regex::Regex::new(r"\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]").unwrap();
    re.replace_all(s, "").to_string()
}
```

### Width/Height Normalization
Force consistent terminal size:
```rust
fn normalized_view(app: &PiApp) -> String {
    app.view_with_size(80, 24)
}
```

## Test Scenarios

### Layout Tests
1. Initial empty state
2. Single user message
3. Single assistant message
4. Conversation with multiple messages
5. Long message with wrapping
6. Scrolled viewport

### State Tests
7. Idle state
8. Streaming text
9. Streaming thinking
10. Tool execution in progress
11. Error display
12. Slash command help

### Input Tests
13. Text in input field
14. Multi-line input
15. History navigation
16. Cursor positioning

## Dependencies
None (can start independently)

## Files
- tests/tui_snapshot.rs
- tests/snapshots/*.snap (generated)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] insta crate integrated
- [ ] ANSI stripping works
- [ ] Terminal size normalized
- [ ] 15+ snapshot tests
- [ ] Logs include snapshot metadata
- [ ] cargo insta test works
- [ ] CI validates snapshots","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:28.554490342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:26:18.038231211Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T08:42:46.796412182Z","close_reason":"Added tui_snapshot tests + insta snapshots; suite green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1d3","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1d3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2526,"issue_id":"bd-1d3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: added TUI snapshot test harness (tests/tui_snapshot.rs) using TestHarness + insta; added PiApp::set_terminal_size and PI_TEST_MODE init gating for animations; added dev-dep insta. Snapshots pending because cargo check/clippy failing due to removed reqwest/tokio deps still referenced in code; rustfmt still fails on import ordering in src/interactive.rs and provider tests.","created_at":"2026-02-03T06:19:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1d5i","title":"E2E Interactive: /settings UI toggles + persistence","description":"# Goal
End-to-end interactive test for `/settings` UI toggles and persistence.

# Scope
- Launch interactive session using the tmux harness from `bd-3hp` (fixed 80x24 capture).
- Toggle key settings (quietStartup, collapseChangelog, hideThinkingBlock, cursor, padding).
- Exit and relaunch to verify settings persisted and applied.

# Logging
- Capture tmux frames, stdout/stderr, and settings.json before/after.
- Log each toggle action and expected UI effect.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic, offline script with artifact-rich logs.
- Clear assertions on persisted settings and visual state.

# Dependencies
- `bd-3hp` tmux capture harness.
- `/settings` parity + persistence (`bd-axuu`).
- Unified JSONL logging spec (`bd-4u9`).

 {"id":"bd-1dd3","title":"Interactive: render /hotkeys from active keybindings","description":"# Goal
Replace the static `/hotkeys` output with a dynamically generated view from the active keybindings.

# Required Behavior
- `/hotkeys` should list shortcuts grouped by category, mirroring the legacy docs (Cursor Movement, Deletion, Text Input, Application, Session, Models & Thinking, Display, Message Queue, Selection).
- If user overrides are loaded, `/hotkeys` must reflect them.

# Nice-to-have (but keep scope tight)
- Show both default and overridden keys (e.g., dim default, bright override).
- Include a hint for where to edit: `~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json`.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/hotkeys` is generated from action catalog + bindings.
- [ ] Output is stable and testable (snapshot).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"opus","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:36:55.925113647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:07.029739164Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:18:42.591862805Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-3qm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-cru","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2937,"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"/hotkeys dynamic rendering implementation complete (bd-1dd3).

**Changes to src/interactive.rs:**

1. **Added `format_hotkeys()` method:**
   - Iterates through all action categories using `ActionCategory::all()`
   - Groups actions by category with display headers
   - Shows key bindings from the active keybindings (supports user overrides)
   - Includes config file path for user reference
   - Skips empty categories (actions without bindings)

2. **Updated SlashCommand::Hotkeys handler:**
   - Replaced static string with dynamic `self.format_hotkeys()` call
   - Output now reflects actual active keybindings including user overrides

**Output format:**
```
Keyboard Shortcuts
==================

Config: ~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json

## Cursor Movement

  up                   Move cursor up
  down                 Move cursor down
  left, ctrl+b         Move cursor left
  ...

## Application

  escape               Cancel / abort
  ctrl+c               Clear editor
  ...
```

**Test added (tests/tui_state.rs):**
- `tui_state_slash_hotkeys_shows_dynamic_keybindings` - verifies output contains bindings and descriptions

**Tests: All 51 TUI tests passing, 207+ total**
","created_at":"2026-02-03T20:18:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1djr","title":"Set coverage targets per extension type","description":"# Goal
Define explicit coverage targets so the final set spans all extension shapes and high‑value categories.

# Deliverables
- Minimum counts per type (skills, prompts, tools, MCP servers, providers, templates, bundles).
- Category coverage (e.g., search, codegen, devops, data, infra, UI, analytics).
- Rationale tying targets to user value and runtime capabilities.

# Notes
Targets prevent popularity bias from dominating the selection.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:26.684401576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:03:17.160455837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:03:17.160388572Z","close_reason":"Defined Tier-0/Tier-1 coverage targets per extension shape and behavior buckets in EXTENSIONS.md (§1C.5)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1djr","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3853,"issue_id":"bd-1djr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: A ranked list can still miss key extension types.

Reasoning: Explicit coverage targets guarantee we validate the full breadth of extension shapes and user workflows.

Considerations: Targets should be achievable given available artifacts and runtime capabilities.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:53Z"},{"id":3854,"issue_id":"bd-1djr","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"COVERAGE TARGETS: SCALE + QUOTAS

In addition to per-shape minimums, set an explicit overall size target:
- Tier-1 corpus size target: >= 200 extensions

Quotas (example framing)
- Ensure Tier-1 has enough of each high-value shape to exercise runtime thoroughly:
  - tool-only, command, event hooks, UI/RPC, provider registration
  - deps-heavy multi-file packages
  - exec/http/fs heavy extensions

Why this improves user outcomes
- Users care that “extensions people actually use” work. Size + stratified quotas reduce the chance we miss a major class of real-world behavior.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:10:48Z"}]}

  - number of queued follow-up messages

- Prefer consistent styling (dim for follow-up vs bright for steering, etc.).

 {"id":"bd-2q00","title":"E2E: Full agent loop — message → provider → tool → response","description":"Create E2E integration tests that exercise the full agent loop (src/agent.rs). Tests: (1) simple_conversation: send a user message, get assistant response, no tools. (2) tool_round_trip: send a message that triggers read tool (e.g. 'Read the file Cargo.toml and tell me the package name'), verify the agent calls the tool and returns a response incorporating the tool result. (3) multi_tool: send a message triggering multiple tool calls in sequence. (4) bash_tool_e2e: verify bash tool execution through the agent loop (e.g. 'Run echo hello'). (5) error_recovery: send a message with an invalid tool call and verify the agent handles the error gracefully. Use Anthropic as the default provider (most reliable). All tests log the full message exchange, timing per turn, total tokens used.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedPuma","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:12:02.833555180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:27:50.022721208Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:27:50.022689800Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q00","depends_on_id":"bd-18j1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q00","depends_on_id":"bd-ovmd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q00","depends_on_id":"bd-yqfx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2q2v","title":"Unit: CLI input plumbing (stdin + @file + args)","description":"# Goal
Ensure CLI input plumbing is fully covered without mocks, including piped stdin, @file expansion, and message arg ordering.

# Why / User Impact
Input composition bugs create wrong prompts, missing context, or leaked sessions. These tests guarantee predictable input handling.

# Scope (Granular)
- `prepare_initial_message` + `process_file_arguments` with mixed @file + message args.
- Piped stdin vs explicit message args in print mode (`-p`), including empty stdin.
- Image auto-resize flags and error paths for missing/unsupported files.
- Verify **no session side-effects** when `--no-session` / `-p` is used.
- Confirm CLI rejects incompatible modes (e.g., @file in RPC mode).

# Logging / Artifacts
- Use TestHarness; capture created files + stdout/stderr where applicable.
- Log effective composed message (text + image count) for each case.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Tests in `tests/main_cli_selection.rs` or new `tests/cli_inputs.rs`.
- No mocks/fake providers; offline and deterministic.
- Covers both success and error paths with explicit assertions.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:18.482405934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:24.956919185Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:24.956854875Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q2v","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2q73","title":"E2E: Library integration (markdown/syntax/structured-concurrency)","description":"End-to-end verification of library integration with detailed logging.

## Scenarios

### 1. Markdown Rendering E2E
- Agent response with markdown headers, lists, code blocks
- Verify Console output matches expected styling
- Log: render_start, markdown_parse, style_apply, render_complete

### 2. Syntax Highlighting E2E  
- Agent response with code blocks in multiple languages
- Verify syntax tokens colored correctly
- Log: syntax_detect_language, tokenize_start, tokenize_complete

### 3. Structured Concurrency E2E
- Load extension with async event handlers
- Trigger Ctrl+C during handler execution
- Verify cleanup within budget (5s)
- Verify no orphaned tasks
- Log: region_enter, task_spawn, cancellation_start, task_await, region_exit

## JSONL Logging Format
```json
{"ts": "...", "component": "library", "event": "...", "duration_ms": N, "data": {...}}
```

## Artifact Output
- logs/e2e_library_{timestamp}.jsonl
- Captured terminal output for visual inspection

## Files

- Each extension may expose tools, slash commands, and event hooks; we must test them consistently.

- For each sampled extension, enumerate available features (tools/commands/events).
- Define minimal smoke scenarios + edge cases (inputs, expected outputs).
- Document any required configuration or secrets (mocked).

- A scenario specification mapped to each extension in the sample manifest.
